basics.name: Abhishek Saikia
basics.label: Systems and Software Developer
basics.summary: I am a final year Computer science student, currently based in India. Currently seeking opportunities in backend and infra roles.

work.name: Rablo.in
highlights: Improved website performance by optimizing code
responsibilities: Built an event-driven email automation service in Node.js handling onboarding workflows, eliminating manual steps in the activation pipeline and measurably improving activation rates.
Designed REST API workflows and server-side state architecture, reducing client response latency by ~15% through improved request handling and payload structure.
Optimized production frontend performance via code-splitting, memoization, and SSR tuning, improving page load times by ~20%.
Resolved 50+ bugs across frontend and backend codebases, improving CI/CD pipeline stability and reducing build failures in production.

opensource.name: Go Athens
opensource.summary: Identified an observability gap: Athens exposed HTTP-level OpenCensus metrics but lacked proxy-layer cache and upstream fetch metrics, limiting operator visibility into cache effectiveness and upstream reliability.
contributions: Implemented three additive OpenCensus metrics in Go under pkg/observ: cache lookup total (Counter; labels: hit/miss + cache type), upstream fetch total (Counter; labels: success/failure), and upstream fetch duration seconds (Histogram, exponential buckets).
Designed with low-cardinality labels and zero API/storage changes; integrates with existing Prometheus, Datadog, and Stackdriver exporters. Added unit tests for all three metric paths

opensource.name: go-swagger
opensource.summary: Identified a runtime panic in the schema parser where comment-based enum declarations assumed a non-empty type slice, causing crashes during spec generation when the type was uninitialized.
contributions: Fixed a slice out-of-range panic by guarding access to sv.current.Type[0] and safely handling cases where the schema type was not yet initialized.
Preserved existing parsing behavior by passing an empty type to enum parsing logic when unavailable, ensuring backward compatibility.
Added a regression test covering the full Run() path with a swagger:model struct using comment-based enum declarations, reproducing the crash before the fix and validating correctness after.

projects.name: Craw
projects.description: A developer-focused custom high-performance web crawler in Go.
highlights: Built a production-grade web crawler in Go with distributed-system design patterns — Redis-backed crawl frontier, concurrent worker pipelines, and SimHash deduplication — achieving 3.16 pages/sec sustained over 3+ hours.

Designed a full crawling pipeline including URL frontier (scheduler with retry + visibility timeout), fetcher with rate limiting and robots.txt compliance, parser for HTML/Markdown extraction, and storage layer using JSON, Redis, and MinIO.

projects.name: VaultGuard
highlights: Built a consent-driven financial data sharing platform in TypeScript using a microservices architecture with REST API inter-service communication, isolated via Docker Compose.
Implemented consent enforcement and access controlsing Prisma ORM with PostgreSQL, JWT auth, role-based authorization, and audit-logging middleware to prevent unauthorized cross-tenant access.
